author:	coderabbitai
association:	contributor

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: summarize by coderabbit.ai -->
<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: review paused by coderabbit.ai -->

> [!NOTE]
> ## Reviews paused
> 
> It looks like this branch is under active development. To avoid overwhelming you with review comments due to an influx of new commits, CodeRabbit has automatically paused this review. You can configure this behavior by changing the `reviews.auto_review.auto_pause_after_reviewed_commits` setting.
> 
> Use the following commands to manage reviews:
> - `@coderabbitai resume` to resume automatic reviews.
> - `@coderabbitai review` to trigger a single review.
> 
> Use the checkboxes below for quick actions:
> - [ ] <!-- {"checkboxId": "7f6cc2e2-2e4e-497a-8c31-c9e4573e93d1"} --> ▶️ Resume reviews
> - [ ] <!-- {"checkboxId": "e9bb8d72-00e8-4f67-9cb2-caf3b22574fe"} --> 🔍 Trigger review

<!-- end of auto-generated comment: review paused by coderabbit.ai -->
<!-- walkthrough_start -->

<details>
<summary>📝 Walkthrough</summary>

## Walkthrough

Adds automatic cover fetching (MusicBrainz/Last.fm) with configurable strategy, preserves manual uploads via Album.manual_cover, wires a non-wasm CoverFetcher into the scanner, exposes AppConfig flags, adjusts server sync merge/persistence, and adds a desktop UI handler for manual cover uploads.

## Changes

**Album Cover Auto-Fetch and Manual Upload Feature**

|Layer / File(s)|Summary|
|---|---|
|**Data Model & Configuration Contracts** <br> `crates/reader/src/models.rs`, `crates/reader/src/metadata.rs`, `crates/config/src/lib.rs`|Adds `Album.manual_cover: bool` with `#[serde(default)]`, updates `Library::add_album` merge logic to respect `manual_cover`, initializes `manual_cover = false` in album construction paths, and introduces `FetchStrategy` plus `AppConfig` fields (`auto_fetch_covers`, `cover_fetch_strategy`).|
|**Server Sync Merge & Persistence** <br> `crates/pages/src/server/subsonic_sync.rs`|Adds `normalize_album_id`, sets `manual_cover=false` for constructed server albums, changes Jellyfin/Subsonic/custom sync write-back to per-album merge preserving prior `cover_path`/`manual_cover`, and adds tracing progress logs.|
|**Cover Fetcher Module Implementation** <br> `crates/reader/Cargo.toml`, `crates/reader/src/lib.rs`, `crates/reader/src/cover_fetcher.rs`|New non-wasm `cover_fetcher` module: `CoverFetcher` with reqwest client, cache dir, FetchStrategy/Last.fm key, MusicBrainz and Last.fm backends, 1100ms rate limiting, image download and `save_cover`, and `FetchReport`. Adds workspace deps and cfg gating.|
|**Scanner Integration & Wiring** <br> `crates/reader/src/scanner.rs`|Extends `scan_directory` signature with `auto_fetch_covers`, `fetch_strategy`, `lastfm_api_key`; optionally constructs `CoverFetcher` after scanning and awaits `fetch_missing_covers`, logging found/missing/errors.|
|**Application Configuration Binding** <br> `crates/kopuz/src/main.rs`|Rescan code derives cover-fetch settings from `AppConfig` (auto-fetch flag, fetch strategy, Last.fm key) and passes them to `reader::scan_directory`.|
|**Desktop Manual Cover Upload** <br> `crates/components/src/album_details.rs`|Adds desktop-only `on_cover_click` handler that opens image picker, reads file bytes, saves cover via `reader::utils::save_cover` into cover cache, updates album `cover_path` and sets `manual_cover = true`, and persists `library.json` with rollback on failure.|

## Sequence Diagram

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
  participant App
  participant AppConfig
  participant Scanner
  participant CoverFetcher
  participant MusicBrainz
  participant LastFm
  participant Library
  App->>AppConfig: read auto_fetch_covers, cover_fetch_strategy, lastfm_api_key
  App->>Scanner: scan_directory(dir, cache_dir, ..., auto_fetch_covers, cover_fetch_strategy, lastfm_api_key)
  Scanner->>Scanner: scan for audio files, build Library
  alt auto_fetch_covers enabled
    Scanner->>CoverFetcher: new(cache_dir, strategy, api_key, progress_cb)
    CoverFetcher->>Library: iterate albums with cover_path == None (skip Unknown Album/manual_cover)
    loop per missing album
      CoverFetcher->>CoverFetcher: choose backend by FetchStrategy
      alt MusicBrainz path
        CoverFetcher->>MusicBrainz: search/resolve release_id, request Cover Art Archive
        MusicBrainz-->>CoverFetcher: image metadata
      else LastFm path
        CoverFetcher->>LastFm: album.getinfo(api_key)
        LastFm-->>CoverFetcher: image URLs
      end
      CoverFetcher->>CoverFetcher: download selected image bytes
      CoverFetcher->>Library: save_cover, update album.cover_path and manual_cover
    end
    CoverFetcher-->>Scanner: FetchReport { found, missing, errors }
    Scanner->>Scanner: log results
  end
  Scanner-->>App: scan complete
```

## Estimated code review effort

🎯 4 (Complex) | ⏱️ ~45 minutes

## Possibly related issues

- Kopuz-org/kopuz#271: Implements the auto-fetch/manual-cover feature requested in this issue (cover_fetcher, scanner wiring, AppConfig fields, Album.manual_cover).

## Possibly related PRs

- [Kopuz-org/kopuz#205](https://github.com/Kopuz-org/kopuz/pull/205): Modifies `reader::scanner::scan_directory`; changes likely overlap with scanner wiring.  
- [Kopuz-org/kopuz#206](https://github.com/Kopuz-org/kopuz/pull/206): Related AppConfig Last.fm credential changes that interact with the new Last.fm wiring.  
- [Kopuz-org/kopuz#209](https://github.com/Kopuz-org/kopuz/pull/209): Alters post-scan artwork handling and may overlap with cover persistence logic.

## Suggested labels

`enhancement`

## Suggested reviewers

- temidaradev
- Irilith

> MusicBrainz hummed through the night,  
> Last.fm shared an artful clue,  
> Albums now wear artwork new,  
> A rabbit saved the picture right." 🐰🎶

author:	coderabbitai

**Actionable comments posted: 3**

<details>
<summary>🤖 Prompt for all review comments with AI agents</summary>

```
Verify each finding against current code. Fix only still-valid issues, skip the
rest with a brief reason, keep changes minimal, and validate.

Inline comments:
In `@crates/components/src/album_details.rs`:
- Around line 63-80: The code currently writes the cover file
(reader::utils::save_cover), mutates the in-memory album (library.write() ->
lib.albums -> album.cover_path = Some(saved); album.manual_cover = true) and
then drops the result of library.read().save(&lib_path), losing failures
silently; update this flow so that after save_cover and after mutating the album
you call library.read().save(&lib_path) and check its Result: on Ok keep the
mutation; on Err log or return the error (surface it to the caller/UI) and roll
back both the in-memory mutation (reset album.cover_path and album.manual_cover
to their previous values) and the persisted cache (delete the saved cover file
at saved) to avoid orphaned files, using the same identifiers
(reader::utils::save_cover, saved,
library.write()/lib.albums/album.cover_path/album.manual_cover,
library.read().save, cover_cache, lib_path) to locate the spots to implement the
error handling and rollback.

In `@crates/reader/src/cover_fetcher.rs`:
- Around line 138-149: The CoverArchiveImage struct and selection logic assume a
string "type" field but the API uses a boolean "front" and a "types" array;
update the CoverArchiveImage definition (used when deserializing
CoverArchiveResponse from resp.json()) to include front: Option<bool> and types:
Option<Vec<String>> (retain image: String) with appropriate serde field names,
then change the selection logic on body.images to first prefer images where
front == Some(true) or where types contains "Front" (e.g., i.front == Some(true)
|| i.types.as_ref().map_or(false, |ts| ts.contains(&"Front".to_string()))), and
fall back to body.images.first() if none match.
- Line 199: The Last.fm API call uses plaintext HTTP in the .get call; update
the request URL to use HTTPS by changing the string in the
.get("http://ws.audioscrobbler.com/2.0/") call to
"https://ws.audioscrobbler.com/2.0/". Also search the CoverFetcher code (e.g.,
methods in cover_fetcher.rs that build the request) for any other
"http://ws.audioscrobbler.com" occurrences and replace them with the HTTPS
endpoint to ensure the API key and responses are transmitted securely.
```

</details>

author:	coderabbitai

**Actionable comments posted: 4**

<details>
<summary>♻️ Duplicate comments (2)</summary><blockquote>

<details>
<summary>crates/reader/src/cover_fetcher.rs (2)</summary><blockquote>

`197-205`: _⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🟠 Major_ | _⚡ Quick win_

**Use HTTPS for the Last.fm API call.**

This request still uses plain HTTP, so the API key goes over the wire unencrypted. The `album.getInfo` documentation links its example request through `https://ws.audioscrobbler.com/2.0/`. ([last.fm](https://www.last.fm/fr/api/show/album.getInfo))

<details>
<summary>🤖 Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

```
Verify each finding against current code. Fix only still-valid issues, skip the
rest with a brief reason, keep changes minimal, and validate.

In `@crates/reader/src/cover_fetcher.rs` around lines 197 - 205, The Last.fm API
call is using plain HTTP in the
self.client.get("http://ws.audioscrobbler.com/2.0/") call; update that URL to
use HTTPS (https://ws.audioscrobbler.com/2.0/) so the api_key and other params
are sent encrypted, i.e., change the string passed to self.client.get(...) in
cover_fetcher.rs (the album.getinfo request built with .query(...)) to use
https://.
```

</details>

---

`137-149`: _⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🟠 Major_ | _⚡ Quick win_

**Match the documented Cover Art Archive image schema.**

The Cover Art Archive marks front images with `front` and `types`, not a singular `type` field, so this deserializer can miss or reject valid front-cover responses. ([musicbrainz.org](https://musicbrainz.org/doc/Cover_Art_Archive/API?utm_source=openai))

<details>
<summary>🤖 Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

```
Verify each finding against current code. Fix only still-valid issues, skip the
rest with a brief reason, keep changes minimal, and validate.

In `@crates/reader/src/cover_fetcher.rs` around lines 137 - 149, The
CoverArchiveImage deserializer and selection logic are incorrect: replace the
single image_type field in struct CoverArchiveImage with fields matching the API
schema (e.g., front: Option<bool> and types: Option<Vec<String>> along with
image: String), then change the selection logic that builds url (the .find
closure) to detect front images by checking either front == Some(true) or that
types contains "Front" (case-insensitive as desired), falling back to
body.images.first() if none match; update any references to image_type
accordingly.
```

</details>

</blockquote></details>

</blockquote></details>

<details>
<summary>🤖 Prompt for all review comments with AI agents</summary>

```
Verify each finding against current code. Fix only still-valid issues, skip the
rest with a brief reason, keep changes minimal, and validate.

Inline comments:
In `@crates/config/src/lib.rs`:
- Around line 564-565: The serde default for the auto_fetch_covers field
currently enables third‑party cover lookups for existing configs; change the
default to opt‑out by updating the default provider used by #[serde(default =
"default_auto_fetch_covers")] so that default_auto_fetch_covers() returns false
(or replace the attribute to point to a new default function returning false),
and run/update any tests or docs that assume the old true default to reflect the
new opt‑out behavior for the auto_fetch_covers field.

In `@crates/kopuz/src/main.rs`:
- Around line 1339-1341: The Last.fm API key variable lastfm_key (and the logic
that checks it when deciding to enable the Last.fm fallback) currently treats
whitespace-only strings as set; change the check to treat whitespace-only keys
as unset by trimming before emptiness checks (e.g., use
lastfm_key.trim().is_empty() or work with lastfm_key.as_str().trim()) wherever
the code presently uses !k.is_empty() or similar—update the logic around
fetch_covers/fetch_strategy/lastfm_key usage so whitespace-only keys disable the
Last.fm fallback just like an empty string.

In `@crates/reader/src/cover_fetcher.rs`:
- Around line 57-61: The current release_id selection stops at the first track
with a matching album_id even if its musicbrainz_release_id is None; update the
lookup to find the first track where t.album_id == album.id AND
t.musicbrainz_release_id.is_some(), then extract the MBID (as_deref) from that
track. Locate the release_id computation that uses
library.tracks.iter().find(...) and change the predicate to require a present
musicbrainz_release_id before calling and_then/map to as_deref so a later track
with a valid MBID is returned.
- Around line 151-159: The current fetch path calls .bytes() on the response
without checking status, so non-2xx responses (404/5xx) can be treated as
successful; update the request pipeline used in cover_fetcher to call
.send().await, then call .error_for_status() (or .error_for_status_ref()) on the
Response before .bytes() to convert HTTP errors into Err, and propagate that
error so we don't cache error bodies — apply this change where the code does
self.client.get(&url.image).send().await...bytes().await (and the similar
occurrence around the other block referenced) so that only successful 2xx
responses are converted to bytes and returned.

---

Duplicate comments:
In `@crates/reader/src/cover_fetcher.rs`:
- Around line 197-205: The Last.fm API call is using plain HTTP in the
self.client.get("http://ws.audioscrobbler.com/2.0/") call; update that URL to
use HTTPS (https://ws.audioscrobbler.com/2.0/) so the api_key and other params
are sent encrypted, i.e., change the string passed to self.client.get(...) in
cover_fetcher.rs (the album.getinfo request built with .query(...)) to use
https://.
- Around line 137-149: The CoverArchiveImage deserializer and selection logic
are incorrect: replace the single image_type field in struct CoverArchiveImage
with fields matching the API schema (e.g., front: Option<bool> and types:
Option<Vec<String>> along with image: String), then change the selection logic
that builds url (the .find closure) to detect front images by checking either
front == Some(true) or that types contains "Front" (case-insensitive as
desired), falling back to body.images.first() if none match; update any
references to image_type accordingly.
```

</details>

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment by CodeRabbit for review status -->

author:	Irilith

can you check coderabbit review, if it valid look into it, if not skip.
--
author:	coderabbitai
association:	contributor
edited:	false
status:	commented
--
**Actionable comments posted: 1**

> [!CAUTION]
> Some comments are outside the diff and can’t be posted inline due to platform limitations.
> 
> 
> 
> <details>
> <summary>⚠️ Outside diff range comments (1)</summary><blockquote>
> 
> <details>
> <summary>crates/pages/src/server/subsonic_sync.rs (1)</summary><blockquote>
> 
> `179-210`: _⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🟠 Major_ | _⚡ Quick win_
> 
> **Preserve manual server covers when refreshing `jellyfin_albums`.**
> 
> This writeback path bypasses the new `Library::add_album` merge rules and rebuilds server albums with `manual_cover: false`. Any manual upload on a Jellyfin/Subsonic album gets dropped on the next sync, especially in the `clear_first` and full-replacement paths.
> 
> <details>
> <summary>🛠️ Suggested direction</summary>
> 
> ```diff
> +fn merge_manual_cover(existing: Option<&Album>, mut incoming: Album) -> Album {
> +    if let Some(existing) = existing {
> +        if incoming.cover_path.is_none() || existing.manual_cover {
> +            incoming.cover_path = existing.cover_path.clone();
> +        }
> +        if existing.manual_cover {
> +            incoming.manual_cover = true;
> +        }
> +    }
> +    incoming
> +}
> ```
> 
> Use the same merge before pushing Jellyfin albums and before assigning `data.albums` into `lib_write.jellyfin_albums`.
> </details>
> 
> <details>
> <summary>🤖 Prompt for AI Agents</summary>
> 
> ```
> Verify each finding against current code. Fix only still-valid issues, skip the
> rest with a brief reason, keep changes minimal, and validate.
> 
> In `@crates/pages/src/server/subsonic_sync.rs` around lines 179 - 210, The current
> code overwrites lib_write.jellyfin_albums with new vectors (out_albums and
> data.albums), which drops manual_cover edits; instead merge each incoming album
> through the library's existing merge logic (Library::add_album or whatever merge
> helper you have) so manual server covers are preserved—i.e., iterate over
> out_albums and data.albums and call the merge/add method (referencing
> Library::add_album and lib_write.jellyfin_albums/data.albums) rather than
> assigning or naively pushing/replacing the vector.
> ```
> 
> </details>
> 
> </blockquote></details>
> 
> </blockquote></details>

<details>
<summary>🤖 Prompt for all review comments with AI agents</summary>

```
Verify each finding against current code. Fix only still-valid issues, skip the
rest with a brief reason, keep changes minimal, and validate.

Inline comments:
In `@crates/reader/src/cover_fetcher.rs`:
- Around line 167-170: The MusicBrainz query is built by interpolating raw album
and artist into a quoted Lucene string (variables album and artist), which
breaks on embedded quotes or backslashes; before building query in
cover_fetcher.rs escape backslashes and double quotes in both album and artist
(e.g., replace "\" with "\\\"" and "\" with "\\\\" in that order) and then use
the escaped values when formatting the query, preserving the existing Unknown
Artist check and query logic.

---

Outside diff comments:
In `@crates/pages/src/server/subsonic_sync.rs`:
- Around line 179-210: The current code overwrites lib_write.jellyfin_albums
with new vectors (out_albums and data.albums), which drops manual_cover edits;
instead merge each incoming album through the library's existing merge logic
(Library::add_album or whatever merge helper you have) so manual server covers
are preserved—i.e., iterate over out_albums and data.albums and call the
merge/add method (referencing Library::add_album and
lib_write.jellyfin_albums/data.albums) rather than assigning or naively
pushing/replacing the vector.
```

</details>

<summary>📒 Files selected for processing (10)</summary>

In `@crates/pages/src/server/subsonic_sync.rs`:
- Around line 224-234: The matching logic uses album.id directly so
Subsonic/Custom IDs that append a cover-tag suffix won't match prior state;
update the merge to use a stable album identity by normalizing IDs before
comparing (e.g., implement and use a helper like normalize_album_id or compute a
stable key from title/artist/album_id_base that strips the cover-tag suffix)
when iterating lib_write.jellyfin_albums and when building Subsonic/Custom IDs
(the block that constructs those IDs at lines ~291-295). Replace direct
comparisons of album.id/old.id with comparisons of the normalized key (or
compute the stable key once and attach it to the album struct) so manual_cover
and cover_path preservation survive resyncs.
```

<summary>🤖 Prompt for all review comments with AI agents</summary>

```
Verify each finding against current code. Fix only still-valid issues, skip the
rest with a brief reason, keep changes minimal, and validate.

Inline comments:
In `@crates/pages/src/server/subsonic_sync.rs`:
- Around line 266-276: When replacing an album in the non-clear merge path
(inside the branch that finds index in lib_write.jellyfin_albums and assigns
lib_write.jellyfin_albums[index] = new_album), also update any tracks in
lib_write.jellyfin_tracks whose album_id points to the old album id so they
reference the new_album.id (use the same normalize_album_id comparison used to
find the album). Apply the same update in the parallel merge path around the
other replacement (the block noted by the reviewer at the region analogous to
lines 282–285) so Track.album_id values remain aligned after the album id
rewrite.
- Around line 247-251: When clear_first is true the code currently clears
jellyfin_albums/tracks/genres but only extends server_artist_images, which
leaves stale artist image entries; change the logic in the clear branch to
replace lib_write.server_artist_images (not extend) with the new collection so
the artist images are reset alongside albums/tracks/genres — update the same
pattern used later around the server_artist_images handling (also referenced at
the other occurrence around line ~290) so both code paths assign/replace
lib_write.server_artist_images instead of extending it.
```
